objections.text: I was using 2% minoxidil for hair loss and I started getting headaches and dizzy and found they were side effects of people who were over 65 and since I’m 81 so I stopped. Sue me because I’d still like to look good 😂.

I'm a male with male pattern hair loss and thinning. I stopped using liquid minoxidil because of the irritation and flaky scalp which i never had before. I've been using this serum for just a couple of days and already noticing less to no shedding. i definitely use more than a couple of drops to get proper coverage and rub/massage it thoroughly which i feel like not everyone is doing.
Thank you for going over the ingredients. I really appreciate it since now we know how important those ingredients are and their side effects 😊

I have several autoimmune conditions. So when my hair started falling out again, after it had come back for the past year, I thought it was just a side effect I had to live with. 
Then I remembered I hadn't taken any collagen and zinc after my last bottle was finished 4 months ago.
Two DAYS of taking it and my hair loss has stopped. Im glad it stopped but could have saved like 25% of my hair. 😏

If you don't mind a little more body hair. As a male I welcomed it. Low dose Oral minoxidil has the same benefits without the scalp irritation. Online companies like strut gives a prescription that they fill too. 
The topical options do leave the hair and scalp a little dry and crispy. 
The combo of internal and external is the key. 
Also something like herbal saw palmetto that blocks dht could also address hair loss at it's cause. Good luck.
Try minoxidil in foam form instead. I had the same issue as you with the liquid form and my dermatologist recommended foam because people see less irritation with it

I take oral minoxidil… the only side effect I have is swelling around my eyes- upper and lower eyelids. My Dermatologist had me stop taking it until the swelling went away, which it did after about a week, when I started back taking the medication I now take it every other day and so far I’ve not had any swelling. Other than that, it’s totally worth it. I regrew the receded hair in my temple areas. I’m very impressed. I use oral and topical solution of both minoxidil and dutasteride fyi. I never went through the shedding phase though… or at least I didn’t notice it if I did. Been on it 6 months.

​ @rubyalanaharris  I would just buy their lash serum. The ingredients, even the preservatives in both are different for a reason. Using a preservative around your eyes that isn't meant for that purpose can cause inflammation, which will turn chronic if you use the product for months, as directed. Not worth it. Your brows would probably be fine though.
@rubyalanaharris ​​⁠never use anything at all on lashes that isn’t approved by or prescribed by ophthalmologists, or at least marketed as safe for that use. They’re your EYES.

positive_signals.text: I've used it 3-4 times a week, in conjunction with a 5 minute scalp massage every day, for about 4 months and my hair is definitely noticeable more dense (mom grabbed my braid and said "Oh my God, your hair has never been this thick!"), and the baby hairs that frame my face are thicker (actually larger hair strands, instead of fine baby wisps), longer, and more plentiful than they've ever been. I'm pleased enough with the results that I bought a 2nd bottle. It's not a quick fix, and you have to use it consistently for several months, but I am happy with it.
I had one bolding spot and started using this, only on that spot, maybe for two months and I think 3 times a week. Worked 100%, bold spot is gone!
I got this to thicken my hair but it actually cured my seb derm too, crazy!! Nothing has EVER worked for my seb derm, no shampoos nothing. So great!
Bless her! She’s doing the Lord’s work
I used it on hairline and got excellent results, regrowth of baby hairs was dramatic. My daughter in law uses it on her clients after  micro-needling. She claims to get excellent results.

I started having amla juice by mixing it with juice from aloe vera twice a day. Regularly trimming the hair also helps. Take care of your lifestyle. Use liitleextraa cocoonnoin organic hair oil shampoo as both are good for hair fall control. If you will do this every day, you will see results in a few weeks time.
I’ve been using this on my eyebrows for the past 2 months. And wow, they’re so much more full now. Just started using on my scalp. We’ll see. I have high hopes and little doubt about the results

I’m a 26 year old woman and have been using this for 2 years now. I swear by this product! I don’t have any hair loss disorders, but have always had very thin, fine hair, especially around my hairline. I originally began using this only on my hairline. I didn’t like leaving it in since it did make my hair greasy, so I put it in a few hours before I showered. I have noticed a huge increase in my hair thickness and density. My baby hairs have grown like crazy to even my hairline, I now look like I have full bangs as it’s growing out. I now put it all through my hair before showering and my hair looks so think and full. It even feels heavy when I wash it. It doesn’t make my hair grow faster, but definitely fuller and a little bit darker too (I’m blonde). I use it about once a week. Definitely my holy grail which I recommend to everyone now 💛

i noticed less hair fall after a continuous three month period of using littleexrtra reactiavate hair serum. I apply it every night and rinse it off with plain water in the morning. I also use cocooonion oil and shampoo once a week and apply an avocado mask every 15 days. Following this routine has really helped, and I'm seeing new hair growth in several areas of my head
I alternate this with rosemary oil each night. You don’t need much at all— I feel like those who feel greasy are using too much. As someone with fine, post menopausal hair, this works for me.

I lost lots of hair due to stress. Started using the ordinary serum a year ago and it worked amazingly ! I get so many compliments about my hair now. And i go through one bottle a month so it doesn’t last a year if you use it every day . I guess it depends on your body , but it worked wonders for me so if someone is considering trying it out, do it for at least 6 months and see how it goes for you.❤

I’ve been using this for at least a year and it works for me. I started using it where I had tension loss from wearing pony tails and then when I saw it was working I started using it throughout my hair. I definitely have more baby hairs along my hair line and the tension loss has filled in. I plan to try it on my eyebrows too.

I used iRestore for 6 months to see impact by itself then I added the Ordinary peptide and I started getting crazy hair growth. 18 months later, my ponytail is twice the size it used to be. I don’t even use the peptide daily. Maybe a few times per week. I still use iRestore but the major boost in growth was after starting the peptide. The bottle lasts me about 9 months or more.

I'm objectively not pretty, I've got wrinkles/sunspots/scars, my hair refuses to behave & goes in all directions, but I have great hair density and I'm just enjoying knowing that there is one product category out there that I definitely do not need lol! Thanks for the day brightener 🌞🌞🌞

I wish this product was a foam so it was easier to apply. I struggle to get even coverage with it without using way too much and essentially wasting product. I do find its best applied to dry hair rather than wet hair, applying to wet hair seems to cause the "greasy look". I used this to help treat stress induced hair loss to speed up my recovery in a budget friendly way along with taking viviscal and did get good results.

I'm using this product at the moment (I have been for about 4-5 months). I started using it when I noticed my hairline was receeding slightly and I wanted to see if this product would help in stopping this.

I apply 4 drops to my scalp and massage a few drops into my hairline before going to bed (to avoid the greasy look, which is one of the only downsides) and repeat this 2-3 times a week. 

I've not only seen a dramatic improvement in my hairline, but also in the reduction of how much of my hair falls out in the shower (it used to be at least a handful each time I washed my hair, this is now only a few strands). This product shocked me in how quickly it began working, my baby hairs in my hairline started popping out of nowhere after about two uses!

I've had multiple compliments on how healthy my hair looks and definitely noticed that my hair feels thicker for the first time in years. 

I should caveat all this by stating I also started taking Wellwoman vitamins around the same time as starting with this product. But the combination of the two has done absolute wonders for my hair, it looks the healthiest it's ever been!

I want to add as someone with really thin straight hair it was so easy to notice results very quickly!! Maybe its harder to notice a difference if you’re hair isnt quite thin. Also it helped with my scalp not being too dry or oily which i love🩷

I used to suffer from hair loss, Irestore pro works but there’s just so much it can do until I use this serum. On my 7th bottle now 😊. The way I use it is to first brush my scalp until warm feeling, then apply the serum with an applicator, coz if I just use the dropper it runs and drips all over my face. I use it every night on dry hair, by the next morning it’s already absorbed and I see non greasy issue. Highly recommend

Dr. Dray, could you please do a makeup tutorial for us? You look amazing without makeup, of course, but you look beaming in this video. Every single video, I learn something, even if I don't think the particular topic applies to me. You have saved me so much money. I could go on and on about how great you are!!❤ Sending you so much love!
I love this serum and it really works for me! I have gone through 2 bottles. Will keep purchasing.
I've been using this for over 2 weeks now. I don't know if it's making my hair dense or not, but at least I i can see a noticable amount of less hair fall. I am absolutely amazed.

I started using this about six weeks ago. I’d used a serum from Ducray before that, but it was discontinued and replaced with a more expensive one. So I decided to try this out. I feel like I’ve gotten more results from this compared to the Ducray (more baby hairs, more thickening) but do notice my hair is greasier quicker. I might start putting less on the way you suggested.

Actually for oily scalp you should try using it when your hair and scalp are completely,i mean completely dry...i used it on wet scalp or wet hair dry scalp nd it trapped the moisture but when i used it after my hair and scalp was completely dry it wasn't greesy at all and it made my hair look thick and made significant improvement in hair growth. Mind you my scalp is very very oily but it still didn't make it oilier. Also the directions on the product also suggest you should apply it on completely dry hair. Try that once
This worked amazingly for me, I have been using minoxidil for years and my dermatologist told me to use minoxidil once a week to maintain results. I introduced this and i saw visible difference.

@pickledginger4574 it’s not BAD she just doesn’t think it works. In the study that said it worked as well as minoxidil, it was tested against a super low percentage of minoxidil that doesn’t work well. So it’s pretty misleading.

I (36 F) have been using this hair serum consistently for about 8 months after I had the worst hair loss of my life due to stress 😞 I only use it every other day as my hair is extremely fine and thin and I don’t want to apply twice before I wash it again (I wash every other day). My hair is absolutely growing back and the first 4 inches on my hair feels significantly thicker than the rest of my hair. It’s hard to say if the serum alone contributed to the regrowth or stopped the shedding but I plan to continue to use it until the new regrowth has gotten longer. I also use the serum along with a scalp massager to gently massage my scalp when I apply it. Again, not sure if it’s the product but since it is relatively inexpensive I don’t mind continuing with it just in case 😊

I’ve been using this product 4 months now (September 2023) it cured my hair loss and dandruff. I use it 4 days a week. I had hair loss on and off since 2019 and had tried lots of supplements but nothing worked. I apply it on my eyelashes as well.

Speaking from experience, it did boosted my hair growth and hair health(?) Dont know how to explain it but my hair looks very good, fuller, easier to comb through with less tangled ends and slightly visibly longer than normal rate. when i was using it. Note i dont have any hair conditions etc so im 100% using it for aesthetic(? is that the right word) purposes. Not because i had issues.

I’m 17 and my hairline receded ( a lot ) , so I went to my trichologist and she suggested me this serum with capixyl,and few vitamin deficiency supplements and in a month , my hair loss has been reduced a lot , idk about hair growth tho , I think it would take time ,but yeah ,I would definitely say use serums with capixyl for hair fall

Out of Topic, but: I'm from Brazil, and about Nívea cream: I just learned that in USA you can have a different version of it, without Eucerit. How strange. In Brazil, we buy it and many grandmas and mothers and millenials use for years and almost without wrinkles, because of Eucerit. We also use/alternates with a whole skincare routine. Nívea creme at night, and sometimes during the day for reaally dry skin. For me, it's the only who calms my face when it's sensible from Tretinoin. I think the majority of us use Nívea cream, face, hands, feet (we use a lot of "rasteirinha", open sandals because of the heat, so our feet gets dry). Even oily skins use it at night (my case and the marjority in Brazil). It would be great if you talk more about Nívea cream and those two versions and about Eucerit.

questions.text: Can we also as a society simply be ok with hair loss? I have androgenetic alopecia for decades now; I’ve tried almost everything out there, I wear a topper but on most days I just want to leave the house without a care in the world. Is that too much to ask? Please let’s normalize and accept hair loss in men and women. The pressure is just too much - mentally, physically and financially exhausting 😢

I had a coworker ( male) mention my beginning hair loss years ago. He thought it was a funny comment. It hurt. I can no longer go out without a wig for fear of someone staring or embarrassing me again. Society may not care but they do notice and at times call attention to it. Very painful. Vicki

mixed_signals.text: I have been suffering from some hair loss and have tried a few different things including this. I used it for about 3 months and I actually noticed that it did perpetuate some new hair growth in my problem areas where the hairs continued to grow even after I stopped using it. At the time, I attributed the new growth to scalp oiling, but lately I’ve been thinking that it was actually this product so I’m considering going back to it. I didn’t find it to be greasy as some people have said, but I have very curly hair so my hair can often use some added moisture. I also found that like Dr. Dray said, it lasts a while which is nice. It didn’t have a smell, didn’t make my scalp tingle or burn, and didn’t cause me any irritation (and I tend to have sensitive skin). So, I agree that for the price point it’s worth a try and The Ordinary products are pretty decent. Hope this helps!
#1 not expensive. #2 no reported side effects. #3 some studies showing results. Looks like a product to try.

​​ @lukasbeacon372  4 months can be up to 4 - 6   inches of growth, the person did not specify how long their braid was. (Edit: this is assuming 1 - 1.5 inch of growth a month. I know my hair regularly grows 1 inch a month and that's me not doing anything special)

For some reason, this product helps with my scalp irritation, especially that feeling you get at your roots when you need to wash your hair. But I didn't notice any change in my hair density, it does give the feeling of thicker hair somehow. The fact that it might soothe some of my scalp irritation might help me regain density?? But I'm not too sure. I have dandruff and folliculitis.

I've been using it for 3 weeks & my scalp is finally mositurized & now has healed up nicely. I also find that I don't need to use too much product on my ends anymore too because it's seriously greasy, even for me.

Ive had the worst hair loss this year. iRestore has helped regrow some parts but not completely prevent the hair loss. It’s also the easiest hair growth thing I’ve done. 
l will also give this a shot. I would like hair but not at the cost of other side effects like there are with minoxidil. On days I dont want to deal with my hair I wear a hair scarf.

I have been using TO hair density for quite awhile.  This product has been a miracle for me.  I found a mister pump that fits on the bottle.  I just section my hair and mist it directly onto my scalp.  I don't think my hair was ever this dense and healthy.  I also use other products to take care of the strength of my strands.  I'm 60 and have my best hair in as long as I can remember!

I use it at least 4 times a week.

I don't massage it in, just lightly mist and go to bed.

Didn't work on my natural grey/white hair. I applied some on a spot that is not growing...but it turned that small section into a yellowy brown. It took a while to grow out. Do a small test area first!

Pray to The Lord Jesus about your issues and first of all be saved by believing of The Death, Burial and Resurrection of The Lord Jesus Christ and confess that He is Lord and become saved by faith alone in Him. Give God all your worries and concerns, there's nothing too small or big for Him because He cares for His children. I use rogain mens foam twice a day with lazer therapy 3x a week by HairMax comb (the ultra comb with more layers) and use good hair care products and safe styles for my hair and still color it myself at home with box dyes that work super well for me. I have been diagnosed with androgenetic alopecia and TE (telogen effluvium) from chronic stress and anxiety from abuse and too many major life changes. I also take necessary medicine for my issues and a prenatal and exercise and drink lots of water. Some things you need a whole 180 to get what you've lost back, your hair, mind and body will thank you and all the Glory Goes to God, I have struggled with hair loss for over six years and finally got answers and treatment needed for me all around, not just my hair. God Bless ❤ 
By the way, the botanical and organic topical and herbal remedies are bogus, I tried for 5 years on that and it never worked and hurt my stomach something crazy and wasted a lot of money and time. Do not buy into that junk unless you truly like using it and it's more expensive in the long run than actual remedies that work.
